hero.badge: Fully anonymous · GDPR compliant · No registration required
hero.title: How the Demo Election Works
hero.subtitle: Experience a complete online election. We offer two demo modes: a fully public demo (no login needed) and an organisation-specific demo for registered members.

modes.title: Two Demo Modes — Which One is Right for You?
modes.subtitle: Both modes walk you through the same 5-step voting process. The difference is how you receive your voting code.

public.title: Public Demo
public.description: The fastest way to experience the voting system. No account needed. Your personal 8-character verification code is displayed directly on the screen as soon as you start.
highlights: Open to everyone — no account required
Voting code shown immediately on screen
Perfect for first-time visitors and live demonstrations

org.title: Organisation Demo
org.description: Simulates a real organisational election. You must be logged in as a member. Once you start, the system sends your personal voting code to your registered email address — just like a live election.
highlights: Requires a member account and login
Voting code delivered to your registered email
Mirrors the exact flow of a live election
Ideal for committee members and staff training

steps_intro.title: The 5-Step Voting Process
steps_intro.subtitle: Both demo modes follow the same secure 5-step process used in every real election.

overview.title: Your path through the demo election

step1.title: Enter Your Voting Code
step1.description: You receive a unique 8-character personal code. In the Public Demo it is displayed directly on screen. In the Organisation Demo it is sent to your registered email address.
step1.note: The code is only valid for this session and expires after 60 minutes. Keep it safe — you will need it again at Step 4.

step2.title: Accept the Voting Agreement
step2.description: Read the election rules and confirm your agreement. This is an important legal step in every democratic election.
step2.checkbox_example: I agree to the voting rules and accept the terms and conditions.

step3.title: Select Your Candidates
step3.description: Choose your preferred candidates for each position. There are national positions (visible to all voters) and regional positions (filtered by your registered location).
step3.no_vote_body: You may abstain on any individual position. Your ballot remains valid — you do not have to vote for every position.

step4.title: Review & Confirm Your Vote
step4.description: Check your selections carefully. Then enter your voting code a second time to confirm. This final step is identical in both demo modes.
step4.two_codes_body: Entering your code a second time proves that the same person who opened the ballot is also finalising it. This prevents session hijacking — even if someone intercepts your session, they cannot submit a vote without your code.

step5.title: Done — Your Vote is Counted
step5.description: After confirmation you receive a unique 12-character receipt code. Use it at any time to verify that your vote was correctly recorded.
step5.receipt_caption: Your personal receipt code — 12 characters, unique and anonymous
step5.anon_body: The receipt code is stored as a cryptographic hash. Your name, email address, or any personal data is never linked to your vote. The code lets you verify the record without revealing how you voted.
step5.verify_note: Enter your 12-character receipt code to confirm your vote is in the system.

items.question: What is the difference between the Public Demo and the Organisation Demo?
items.answer: The Public Demo is open to everyone without any login. Your voting code is shown directly on screen — you can start in seconds. The Organisation Demo requires a member login and sends the code to your registered email address, exactly as in a live election.

items.question: Do I need to create an account to try the demo?
items.answer: Not for the Public Demo. Simply click "Start Public Demo" and you are taken straight to the voting screen. For the Organisation Demo you need an existing member account and must be logged in.

items.question: Is my vote truly anonymous?
items.answer: Yes. Neither demo mode stores any link between your identity and your ballot. Votes are recorded with a one-way cryptographic hash only. Even platform administrators cannot see how you voted.

items.question: Can I vote more than once in the demo?
items.answer: Each browser session allows one completed demo vote. You can restart the demo at any time with a fresh session — for example by using a private/incognito browser window.

items.question: How is the Organisation Demo different from a real election?
items.answer: The only difference is the data: the Organisation Demo uses sample candidates and posts. The entire 5-step flow, the code system, the anonymity guarantees, and the receipt verification are identical to a live production election.

cta.title: Ready to experience secure online voting?
cta.subtitle: Choose the demo that suits you best — no commitment, no data stored permanently.
